queries.name: booleanResults
queries.sourceSql:
-- Joined boolean projections cover an aliased exact value and a nullable
-- computed value. TypeScript must lift SQLite's numeric representation for
-- both while the native emitters keep their existing fromRow behavior.
SELECT t.id, t.done AS is_done, t.reviewed AS maybe_done
FROM tasks t
JOIN docs d ON d.project_id = t.project_id
ORDER BY t.id
queries.sql:
-- Joined boolean projections cover an aliased exact value and a nullable
-- computed value. TypeScript must lift SQLite's numeric representation for
-- both while the native emitters keep their existing fromRow behavior.
SELECT t.id, t.done AS isDone, t.reviewed AS maybeDone FROM tasks t
JOIN docs d ON d.project_id = t.project_id
ORDER BY t.id
queries.positionalSql: SELECT t.id, t.done AS isDone, t.reviewed AS maybeDone FROM tasks t JOIN docs d ON d.project_id = t.project_id ORDER BY t.id

queries.name: findDocByOrg
queries.sourceSql:
-- Single-statement file with a `-- name:` override: the path default would be
-- docLookup, but the marker renames it verbatim to findDocByOrg.
-- name: findDocByOrg
SELECT id, body
FROM docs
WHERE org_id = :orgId
ORDER BY id
queries.sql:
-- Single-statement file with a `-- name:` override: the path default would be
-- docLookup, but the marker renames it verbatim to findDocByOrg.
-- name: findDocByOrg
SELECT id, body
FROM docs
WHERE org_id = :orgId
ORDER BY id
queries.positionalSql: SELECT id, body FROM docs WHERE org_id = ? ORDER BY id

queries.name: docValueTypes
queries.sourceSql:
-- Native/Rust query-codegen fidelity for bytes, CRDT, and blob references.
SELECT id, attachment, body_doc, remote_blob
FROM docs
WHERE id = :id
queries.sql:
-- Native/Rust query-codegen fidelity for bytes, CRDT, and blob references.
SELECT id, attachment, body_doc AS bodyDoc, remote_blob AS remoteBlob FROM docs
WHERE id = :id
queries.positionalSql: SELECT id, attachment, body_doc AS bodyDoc, remote_blob AS remoteBlob FROM docs WHERE id = ?

queries.name: docWithBody
queries.sourceSql:
-- Join across docs+tasks (same project); projection mixes both tables.
SELECT d.id AS doc_id, d.body, t.title AS task_title
FROM docs d
JOIN tasks t ON t.project_id = d.project_id
WHERE d.org_id = :orgId
queries.sql:
-- Join across docs+tasks (same project); projection mixes both tables.
SELECT d.id AS docId, d.body, t.title AS taskTitle FROM docs d
JOIN tasks t ON t.project_id = d.project_id
WHERE d.org_id = :orgId
queries.positionalSql: SELECT d.id AS docId, d.body, t.title AS taskTitle FROM docs d JOIN tasks t ON t.project_id = d.project_id WHERE d.org_id = ?

queries.name: docsInProject
queries.sourceSql:
SELECT id, body
FROM docs
WHERE org_id = :orgId AND project_id = :projectId
ORDER BY id
queries.sql:
SELECT id, body
FROM docs
WHERE org_id = :orgId AND project_id = :projectId
ORDER BY id
queries.positionalSql: SELECT id, body FROM docs WHERE org_id = ? AND project_id = ? ORDER BY id

queries.name: hostBoolean
queries.sourceSql:
-- A minimal projection shared by direct, worker/follower, Tauri, and React
-- Native host-parity tests. Each host returns SQLite-shaped 0/1 storage values;
-- the generated runner must return declared JavaScript booleans.
SELECT id, done
FROM tasks
WHERE project_id = :projectId
ORDER BY id
queries.sql:
-- A minimal projection shared by direct, worker/follower, Tauri, and React
-- Native host-parity tests. Each host returns SQLite-shaped 0/1 storage values;
-- the generated runner must return declared JavaScript booleans.
SELECT id, done
FROM tasks
WHERE project_id = :projectId
ORDER BY id
queries.positionalSql: SELECT id, done FROM tasks WHERE project_id = ? ORDER BY id

queries.name: listProjectTasks
queries.sourceSql:
-- Plain select + param inference: :projectId types to project_id (TEXT).
SELECT id, title, done, priority, estimate
FROM tasks
WHERE project_id = :projectId
ORDER BY priority
queries.sql:
-- Plain select + param inference: :projectId types to project_id (TEXT).

FROM tasks
WHERE project_id = :projectId

queries.name: outerJoinValues
queries.sourceSql:
-- Required columns from the optional side must be nullable in every emitted
-- language even though their physical schema columns are NOT NULL.
SELECT t.id AS task_id, t.title, d.body AS doc_body
FROM tasks AS t
LEFT JOIN docs AS d ON d.project_id = t.project_id
WHERE t.id = :taskId
queries.sql:
-- Required columns from the optional side must be nullable in every emitted
-- language even though their physical schema columns are NOT NULL.
SELECT t.id AS taskId, t.title, d.body AS docBody FROM tasks AS t
LEFT JOIN docs AS d ON d.project_id = t.project_id
WHERE t.id = :taskId
queries.positionalSql: SELECT t.id AS taskId, t.title, d.body AS docBody FROM tasks AS t LEFT JOIN docs AS d ON d.project_id = t.project_id WHERE t.id = ?

queries.name: projectDocCount
queries.sourceSql:
-- Aggregate (count(*) → fallback nullable number) + a plain grouped ref.
SELECT project_id, count(*) AS doc_count
FROM docs
GROUP BY project_id
queries.sql:
-- Aggregate (count(*) → fallback nullable number) + a plain grouped ref.
SELECT project_id AS projectId, count(*) AS docCount FROM docs
GROUP BY project_id
queries.positionalSql: SELECT project_id AS projectId, count(*) AS docCount FROM docs GROUP BY project_id

queries.name: reportingTasksByPriority
queries.sourceSql:
-- Nested-folder query: default name is path-derived
-- (reporting/tasks-by-priority.sql → reportingTasksByPriority).
SELECT id, title, priority
FROM tasks
WHERE priority = :priority
ORDER BY id
queries.sql:
-- Nested-folder query: default name is path-derived

SELECT id, title, priority

queries.name: reportOpenTasks
queries.sourceSql:
-- Multi-statement file: two queries in one .sql. Each statement REQUIRES a
-- `-- name:` marker (the file's path can't disambiguate them) and each carries
-- its own leading `-- param`/`-- name` scope.

-- name: reportOpenTasks
-- One statement's scope: infers :projectId from project_id (TEXT).
SELECT id, title, done
FROM tasks
WHERE project_id = :projectId AND done = 0
ORDER BY id
queries.sql:
-- Multi-statement file: two queries in one .sql. Each statement REQUIRES a
-- `-- name:` marker (the file's path can't disambiguate them) and each carries
-- its own leading `-- param`/`-- name` scope.

-- name: reportOpenTasks
-- One statement's scope: infers :projectId from project_id (TEXT).
SELECT id, title, done
FROM tasks
WHERE project_id = :projectId AND done = 0
ORDER BY id
queries.positionalSql: SELECT id, title, done FROM tasks WHERE project_id = ? AND done = 0 ORDER BY id

queries.name: reportDocScores
queries.sourceSql:
-- name: reportDocScores
-- A separate statement, separately scoped: :minScore is compared to an
-- expression, so it needs its own `-- param` comment.
-- param :minScore float
SELECT id, org_id, score
FROM docs
WHERE score > :minScore * 1.0
ORDER BY id
queries.sql:
-- name: reportDocScores
-- A separate statement, separately scoped: :minScore is compared to an
-- expression, so it needs its own `-- param` comment.
-- param :minScore float
SELECT id, org_id AS orgId, score FROM docs
WHERE score > :minScore * 1.0
ORDER BY id
queries.positionalSql: SELECT id, org_id AS orgId, score FROM docs WHERE score > ? * 1.0 ORDER BY id

queries.name: tasksInProjects
queries.sourceSql:
-- IN-clause param inference: both params type to project_id (TEXT).
SELECT id, title FROM tasks WHERE project_id IN (:first, :second)

SELECT id, title FROM tasks WHERE project_id IN (:first, :second)

queries.name: tasksSince
queries.sourceSql:
-- Comment-typed param: :sinceMs isn't compared to a plain column (it hits an
-- expression), so its type is declared explicitly.
-- param :sinceMs integer
SELECT id, title FROM tasks WHERE estimated_at > :sinceMs + 0
queries.sql:
-- Comment-typed param: :sinceMs isn't compared to a plain column (it hits an
-- expression), so its type is declared explicitly.
-- param :sinceMs integer
SELECT id, title FROM tasks WHERE estimated_at > :sinceMs + 0
queries.positionalSql: SELECT id, title FROM tasks WHERE estimated_at > ? + 0

queries.name: joinedSyncCoverage
queries.sourceSql:
select t.id AS taskId, d.id AS docId, d.body from tasks as t
  join docs as d on d.project_id = t.project_id
  where t.project_id = :projectId
    and d.org_id = :orgId
  order by t.id, d.id
queries.sql:
select t.id AS taskId, d.id AS docId, d.body from tasks as t
  join docs as d on d.project_id = t.project_id
  where t.project_id = :projectId
    and d.org_id = :orgId
  order by t.id, d.id
queries.positionalSql: select t.id AS taskId, d.id AS docId, d.body from tasks as t join docs as d on d.project_id = t.project_id where t.project_id = ? and d.org_id = ? order by t.id, d.id

statements.sql:
select t.id AS taskId, d.id AS docId, d.body from tasks as t
  join docs as d on d.project_id = t.project_id
  where t.project_id = :projectId
    and d.org_id = :orgId
  order by t.id, d.id
statements.positionalSql: select t.id AS taskId, d.id AS docId, d.body from tasks as t join docs as d on d.project_id = t.project_id where t.project_id = ? and d.org_id = ? order by t.id, d.id

queries.name: searchTasks
queries.sourceSql:
select id, title, done, priority, estimated_at AS estimatedAt from tasks
  where tasks.project_id = :projectId
    and case when :__syqlActive0 = 0 then 1 else ( (
  title like '%' || :needle || '%'
)) end
    and case when :__syqlActive1 = 0 then 1 else ( priority >= :minPriority) end
    and case when :__syqlActive2 = 0 then 1 else ( done = 0
  ) end order by priority desc, id desc limit min(coalesce(:__syqlLimit, 50), 200)
queries.sql:
select id, title, done, priority, estimated_at AS estimatedAt from tasks
  where tasks.project_id = :projectId
    and case when :__syqlActive0 = 0 then 1 else ( (
  title like '%' || :needle || '%'
)) end
    and case when :__syqlActive1 = 0 then 1 else ( priority >= :minPriority) end
    and case when :__syqlActive2 = 0 then 1 else ( done = 0
  ) end order by priority desc, id desc limit min(coalesce(:__syqlLimit, 50), 200)
queries.positionalSql: select id, title, done, priority, estimated_at AS estimatedAt from tasks where tasks.project_id = ? and case when ? = 0 then 1 else ( ( title like '%' || ? || '%' )) end and case when ? = 0 then 1 else ( priority >= ?) end and case when ? = 0 then 1 else ( done = 0 ) end order by priority desc, id desc limit min(coalesce(?, 50), 200)

statements.sql:

)) end
    and case when :__syqlActive1 = 0 then 1 else ( priority >= :minPriority) end
    and case when :__syqlActive2 = 0 then 1 else ( done = 0
  ) end order by priority asc, id asc limit min(coalesce(:__syqlLimit, 50), 200)

    and 1

  from tasks
  where tasks.project_id = :projectId
    and ( estimated_at between :__syqlRangeStart_range and :__syqlRangeEnd_range)
